title: HistoricalStrategicEvent
description: Dilomatic event record schema v1. Encodes historical political dilemmas: the actors, the strategies they pursued, the outcomes, and scholarly assessment. Text fields use inline citations defined by schemas/inline_citation.json: {{cite|ref_id|pages|quotations}}. All three citation fields are required.

record_id.description: Globally unique identifier. Lowercase hyphenated slug.

schema_version.description: Schema version this record conforms to.

added_at.description: Date this record was first added. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

edited_at.description: Date this record was last edited. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

contributors.description: People who authored or reviewed this record.

title.description: Short descriptive title of the event or dilemma.

alternate_names.description: Other names this event is known by.

geography.description: Locations relevant to the event. Countries, regions, cities, or any geographic descriptor.

iso_country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the badge flag. Optional override when the geography entries are ambiguous, historical, or multi-country. If omitted, the UI guesses from geography entries.

context.description: Scene-setting: who the actors are, why this dilemma arose, and what structural conditions shaped it. 2-5 paragraphs.

triggering_event.description: The specific proximate event that forced the strategic choice. Narrow and concrete — one event, one moment.

timeline.description: Chronological entries marking the datable events of the dilemma — preconditions, the crisis itself, and downstream consequences. Each description supports inline citations using the {{cite|ref|pages|quotations}} syntax.

date.description: Date of the event. Format: YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD. Use the most precise form available.

end_date.description: End date for multi-day or multi-month events. Same format as date. Omit for single-point events.

tag.description: Category bucket for rendering and filtering. precondition = background events predating the crisis; trigger = the proximate event that forced the choice; crisis = the decision window itself; strategy = notable actions taken by actors during the crisis; outcome = the direct result; consequence = downstream effects.

conflict_type.description: Tags describing the type of conflict or political context. Examples: counterinsurgency, counterterrorism, civil-war, interstate-war, hybrid-war, proxy-conflict, covert-action, economic-warfare, information-warfare, election-interference, ethnic-conflict, separatist-conflict, revolutionary-war, decolonization, state-repression, constitutional-crisis, diplomatic-negotiation, sanctions, trade-dispute, institutional-reform, succession-crisis, coalition-politics, peace-process, regime-transition.

summary.description: One-sentence framing of the central strategic dilemma.

category.description: The dilemma expressed as a pair of opposing choices. Examples: [participate, boycott], [negotiate, resist], [escalate, restrain], [intervene, abstain], [separate, unify], [forgive, prosecute], [confront, comply], [reform, continue], [align, isolate].

actors.description: All significant actors in the dilemma. No hierarchy imposed — ordering is presentational only.

description.description: The actor's position, motivations, and structural role in the dilemma.

strategies.description: Flat list of strategies pursued by any actor. Each entry self-describes who pursued it and whether it succeeded, failed, or had mixed results.

outcomes.description: What happened as a result of the dilemma and the strategies pursued.

description.description: Overall narrative of what happened and why.

consequences.description: Structured list of consequences. Each entry describes one consequence and its domain.

domain.description: The sphere in which this consequence manifested. Examples: human, political, institutional, economic, military, intellectual, social, legal.

assessment.description: Editorial analysis, clearly separated from factual outcomes.

verdict.description: Bottom-line judgement: which strategy was superior, why, and with what caveats. This is the only editorial narrative field — it must synthesize AND judge.

historiography.description: Competing scholarly interpretations of the dilemma and its outcome.

interpretation.description: The scholarly reading or framing, with inline citations.

school.description: The intellectual tradition or tendency (e.g. 'party-line', 'Western Marxist', 'liberal-institutionalist').

related_events.description: References to other Dilomatic records that influenced or were influenced by this event.

record_id.description: The record_id of the related event.

relationship.description: How this event relates to the referenced one. Examples: influenced-by, led-to, parallel, response-to, continuation-of.

type.description: Source type. Examples: book, academic-article, documentary, primary-source, government-report, memoir, journalism, website, speech, interview, archive, legal-document, treaty.

author_ids.description: Links to the id of the author(s) in the cited_authors array.

cited_authors.description: Biographical introductions for authors cited in this record.

id.description: Unique slug for the author, e.g., 'rashid-khalidi'

actor.description: Who pursued this strategy. Should match an actor name from the actors array.

effectiveness.description: Whether this strategy achieved its stated aims.

explanation.description: Why this strategy was effective, ineffective, or produced mixed results.

period.description: Time period during which this strategy was actively pursued.

related_strategies.description: Names of other strategies in this record that this one reinforced, contradicted, or responded to.
